question.content: For the sampled Newman conformations at 0, 60, 120, 180, 240, 300, and 360 degrees around the CH2-CH bond, rank their relative energies from highest to lowest. Output exactly one token sequence using labels E0,E60,E120,E180,E240,E300,E360 with '>' for higher-than and '~' for equal-energy, plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Rotate around the CH2-CH bond and classify the seven sampled conformations as E or S.

step_3.content: Compare eclipsed cases: CH3-iPr overlap at 0 degrees is largest, CH3-CH3 at 120 is next, and CH3-H at 240 is smaller.
step_3.key_point: E0 is the highest eclipsed peak.

step_5.content: Among staggered states, anti CH3-iPr at 180 degrees is lowest, then 300 degrees, then 60 degrees.

step_7.content: Connect the sampled points into one periodic qualitative profile from 0 to 360 degrees.
step_7.key_point: 0 and 360 share the same highest level.
